web_text: Paul, Silvanus, and Timothy, to the assembly of the Thessalonians in God our Father, and the Lord Jesus Christ: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ. We are bound to always give thanks to God for you, brothers, even as it is appropriate, because your faith grows exceedingly, and the love of each and every one of you towards one another abounds; so that we ourselves boast about you in the assemblies of God for your perseverance and faith in all your persecutions and in the afflictions which you endure.
slang_text: It's Paul, Silvanus, and Timothy here, writin' to the church of the Thessalonians in God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ. We're bound to always give thanks to God for you, brothers, and fer sure that's just how it oughta be, 'cause your faith's growin' like gangbusters, and every single one of you is totally loving on each other more and more, no lie. So we're out here braggin' on you, like a boss, in God's churches, over how tough you hang and how righteous your faith stays through every persecution and hardship you're puttin' up with.

web_text: This is an obvious sign of the righteous judgment of God, to the end that you may be counted worthy of God’s Kingdom, for which you also suffer. Since it is a righteous thing with God to repay affliction to those who afflict you, and to give relief to you who are afflicted with us, when the Lord Jesus is revealed from heaven with his mighty angels in flaming fire, punishing those who don’t know God, and to those who don’t obey the Good News of our Lord Jesus,
slang_text: This is plain proof, no joke, of God's righteous judgment, so you'd get counted worthy of God's Kingdom, the same Kingdom you're sufferin' for. 'Cause it's only fair, fer sure, for God to pay back the dudes puttin' you through hardship, and toss you some relief, us right along with you, when the Lord Jesus busts outta heaven with His mighty angels, in blazin' fire, dishin' out major payback to whoever doesn't know God and whoever won't obey the Good News of our Lord Jesus,

web_text: who will pay the penalty: eternal destruction from the face of the Lord and from the glory of his might, when he comes in that day to be glorified in his saints, and to be admired among all those who have believed, because our testimony to you was believed. To this end we also pray always for you, that our God may count you worthy of your calling, and fulfill every desire of goodness and work of faith, with power; that the name of our Lord Jesus may be glorified in you, and you in him, according to the grace of our God and the Lord Jesus Christ.
slang_text: and they're gonna pay the price, big-time: eternal destruction, shut out from the Lord's presence and the glory of His might, when He rolls in on that day to get glorified among His saints, admired by every last believer, 'cause our testimony to you actually got believed, fer sure. That's why we're always prayin' for you too — that our God would count you worthy of your callin', and pull off every good desire and faithful deed you got goin', all through His righteous power; so the name of our Lord Jesus gets glorified in you, and you in Him, all through the grace of our God and the Lord Jesus Christ.

web_text: Now, brothers, concerning the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ, and our gathering together to him, we ask you not to be quickly shaken in your mind, and not be troubled, either by spirit, or by word, or by letter as if from us, saying that the day of Christ has already come. Let no one deceive you in any way. For it will not be, unless the rebellion comes first, and the man of sin is revealed, the son of destruction, he who opposes and exalts himself against all that is called God or that is worshiped; so that he sits as God in the temple of God, setting himself up as God.
slang_text: Now, brothers, about our Lord Jesus Christ comin' back and us gettin' gathered up to Him, we're askin' you not to get spooked or shaken up too quick — no way — whether by some so-called spirit, or somethin' somebody said, or a letter claimin' to be from us, sayin' Christ's day's already here. Don't let nobody con you, not one bit, that's totally bogus. 'Cause it ain't happenin' till the rebellion shows up first, and the man of sin gets revealed, that son of destruction, the one who fights against and puffs himself up over everything called God or worshiped as God — sittin' himself right down in God's own temple, actin' like he's God Himself.

web_text: Don’t you remember that, when I was still with you, I told you these things? Now you know what is restraining him, to the end that he may be revealed in his own season. For the mystery of lawlessness already works. Only there is one who restrains now, until he is taken out of the way. Then the lawless one will be revealed, whom the Lord will kill with the breath of his mouth, and destroy by the manifestation of his coming;
slang_text: Don't you remember, when I was still kickin' it with you guys, I already told you this stuff? Now you already know what's holdin' him back, so he'll only get revealed at his own appointed time, fer sure. 'Cause the mystery of lawlessness is already crankin' away, no lie. There's just somebody holdin' it back right now, till he gets outta the way. Then the lawless one's gonna get revealed, and the Lord's gonna totally wipe him out with the breath of His own mouth, and waste him just by showin' up;

web_text: even he whose coming is according to the working of Satan with all power and signs and lying wonders, and with all deception of wickedness for those who are being lost, because they didn’t receive the love of the truth, that they might be saved. Because of this, God sends them a working of error, that they should believe a lie; that they all might be judged who didn’t believe the truth, but had pleasure in unrighteousness.
slang_text: this dude shows up runnin' on Satan's own power, with fake miracles and lyin' wonders, and every kinda wicked con job for the folks headed for destruction, 'cause they never took the truth to heart so they could get saved. That's exactly why God's sendin' 'em somethin' that'll con 'em good, so they'll buy into a bogus lie — so every single one who refused to believe the truth, and got their kicks outta wickedness instead, ends up judged for it, no way around it.

web_text: But we are bound to always give thanks to God for you, brothers loved by the Lord, because God chose you from the beginning for salvation through sanctification of the Spirit and belief in the truth; to which he called you through our Good News, for the obtaining of the glory of our Lord Jesus Christ. So then, brothers, stand firm, and hold the traditions which you were taught by us, whether by word, or by letter. Now our Lord Jesus Christ himself, and God our Father, who loved us and gave us eternal comfort and good hope through grace,
slang_text: But we're bound to always give thanks to God for you, brothers loved by the Lord, 'cause God picked you out from the get-go for salvation, through the Spirit makin' you holy and you believin' the truth, fer sure. He called you to that through our Good News, so you could get a piece of our Lord Jesus Christ's righteous glory. So then, brothers, stand your ground, hold tight to the traditions we taught you, whether by word or by letter. Now may our Lord Jesus Christ Himself, and God our Father, who loved us and handed us eternal comfort and solid hope through grace,

web_text: comfort your hearts and establish you in every good work and word.
slang_text: comfort your hearts and lock you in solid, in every good work and every good word.

web_text: Finally, brothers, pray for us, that the word of the Lord may spread rapidly and be glorified, even as also with you; and that we may be delivered from unreasonable and evil men; for not all have faith. But the Lord is faithful, who will establish you, and guard you from the evil one. We have confidence in the Lord concerning you, that you both do and will do the things we command.
slang_text: Finally, brothers, pray for us, that the Lord's word spreads fast and gets glorified, same as it did with you guys; and that we'd get rescued from bogus, evil dudes — 'cause not everybody's got faith, no lie. But the Lord's faithful, fer sure, and He's gonna keep you solid, guard you from the evil one. We're totally confident in the Lord about you, that you're already doin' and gonna keep doin' what we tell you.

web_text: May the Lord direct your hearts into God’s love, and into the perseverance of Christ. Now we command you, brothers, in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, that you withdraw yourselves from every brother who walks in rebellion, and not after the tradition which they received from us. For you know how you ought to imitate us. For we didn’t behave ourselves rebelliously among you, neither did we eat bread from anyone’s hand without paying for it, but in labor and travail worked night and day, that we might not burden any of you;
slang_text: May the Lord point your hearts straight at God's love and Christ's perseverance. Now we're orderin' you, brothers, in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, to steer clear of every brother who's actin' rebellious, not stickin' to the tradition they got taught by us — that's bogus behavior. You already know how you oughta copy us. We never acted rebellious among you, and we never mooched off nobody's dime without payin' our own way — we worked our tails off, night and day, so we wouldn't burden a single one of you;

web_text: not because we don’t have the right, but to make ourselves an example to you, that you should imitate us. For even when we were with you, we commanded you this: “If anyone will not work, don’t let him eat.” For we hear of some who walk among you in rebellion, who don’t work at all, but are busybodies. Now those who are that way, we command and exhort in the Lord Jesus Christ, that with quietness they work, and eat their own bread.
slang_text: not that we didn't have the right to lean on you — we just wanted to set an example so you'd copy us. Even back when we were with you, we ordered you this: "If somebody won't work, don't let him eat, neither." We're hearin' about some of you actin' rebellious, not workin' at all, just runnin' around bein' nosy busybodies, total wastoids. To folks like that, we're orderin' and pushin' 'em in the Lord Jesus Christ, to quiet down, get to work, and eat their own bread they earned, fer real.

web_text: But you, brothers, don’t be weary in doing what is right. If any man doesn’t obey our word in this letter, note that man, that you have no company with him, to the end that he may be ashamed. Don’t count him as an enemy, but admonish him as a brother. Now may the Lord of peace himself give you peace at all times in all ways. The Lord be with you all.
slang_text: But you, brothers, don't get burned out doin' what's right. If any dude won't obey what we're sayin' in this letter, mark him down, and don't hang out with him, so he'll feel ashamed about it, no lie. Don't treat him like an enemy though, correct him like a brother, fer sure. Now may the Lord of peace Himself give you peace, always, every which way. The Lord be with all of you.

web_text: The greeting of me, Paul, with my own hand, which is the sign in every letter: this is how I write. The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you all. Amen.
slang_text: This is me, Paul, signin' off with my own hand, my signature mark on every letter — this is just how I write. The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with all of you. Amen.
